text: The descriptive claim "tool granularity matters" is already scooped (The Tool Illusion, COLM-2026, web-agent domain). Defensible novelty is reframed to three pillars: a formal-difficulty CSP testbed, a model x granularity interaction, and a predictive cost theory.

bibliography.title: “The Tool Illusion” (COLM 2026)
bibliography.summary: Web-agent study arguing that how tools are framed/granularized changes agent behaviour.
bibliography.relevance: Closest prior work — it already makes the descriptive claim that “tool granularity matters”, so that headline is scooped. Our novelty has to move past description.

bibliography.title: Toolformer (Schick et al., 2023)
bibliography.summary: LMs teach themselves to call external APIs via self-supervised demonstrations.
bibliography.relevance: Establishes that tool use is learnable, but treats the toolset as fixed — granularity is never a controlled variable.

bibliography.title: ReAct (Yao et al., 2022)
bibliography.summary: Interleaves chain-of-thought reasoning with tool actions.
bibliography.relevance: Defines the reason–act loop we operate in, yet says nothing about varying the granularity of the action/tool space.

bibliography.title: Gorilla (Patil et al., 2023)
bibliography.summary: LLM trained to call from a massive catalogue of APIs via retrieval.
bibliography.relevance: Scales the *number* of tools, which is adjacent to granularity, but optimises retrieval rather than isolating granularity as an axis with a difficulty/cost model.

bibliography.title: ToolLLM / ToolBench (Qin et al., 2023)
bibliography.summary: Benchmark of 16k+ real-world APIs for tool-use agents.
bibliography.relevance: Big tool spaces, but granularity is entangled with task variety; no controlled granularity sweep on a fixed problem family.

bibliography.title: Berkeley Function-Calling Leaderboard (BFCL, 2024)
bibliography.summary: Standard evaluation of function-calling accuracy across models.
bibliography.relevance: Measures tool-calling quality but not granularity-as-a-knob — confirms the community lacks a granularity-controlled testbed, which is the gap we fill.
